title: Why Deploy on the Edge?
concept: Edge deployment means running AI models directly on local devices like phones, cameras, or sensors instead of relying on remote cloud servers.

exercises.prompt: Recap: What is latency in a machine learning system?

options.text: The time it takes to get a prediction for a single input
The memory size of the weights in megabytes
The volume of data processed in a given time frame

exercises.explain: Latency measures the delay for a single request to finish.

cards.title: No internet, no problem
cards.body: If you are using a smart camera in a remote forest to monitor wildlife, there is no Wi-Fi or 5G to send images to the cloud. The camera has to process the images right there, on the device itself. This is edge computing!

cards.title: What is Edge Deployment?
cards.body: Edge deployment is the practice of running neural networks directly on local hardware (the "edge") rather than sending data to centralized cloud servers. It relies on local CPUs, GPUs, or specialized AI accelerators.

cards.title: Cooking at home vs. ordering takeout
cards.body: Think of edge computing like cooking at home: you have the food immediately without waiting for a delivery driver (low latency, works offline). Cloud computing is like ordering takeout: it might be a fancier meal, but you must wait for the delivery, and if the road is blocked, you get nothing.

cards.title: Smart home doorbells
cards.body: A smart doorbell uses an edge processor to detect people. Because it doesn't upload video to the cloud for analysis, it alerts you instantly and saves significant internet bandwidth.

cards.title: Edge is not always less capable
cards.body: You might think edge devices can only run simple, inaccurate models. In reality, modern smartphones and edge chips have dedicated neural processors that run complex models in milliseconds.

cards.title: Edge vs. Cloud Processing
cards.body: Where the AI math actually happens:

left.text:
Runs locally on device
Zero internet needed
Ultra-low latency
Strict memory limits

right.text:
Runs on remote servers
Requires constant internet
Network delay (latency)
Unlimited compute & size

exercises.prompt: Which of the following is a primary benefit of deploying a model on the edge?

options.text: It can run offline without an internet connection
It has access to infinite storage and server memory
It automatically corrects labeled data errors
It eliminates the need for any model optimization

exercises.explain: Edge models run locally, so they do not require an active network connection to make predictions.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Edge devices have strict power and memory limitations compared to cloud servers.
exercises.explain: True. Phones and IoT devices run on batteries and have limited RAM, requiring lightweight models.

exercises.sentence: Running a model directly on a user's smartphone is an example of ___ deployment.
exercises.explain: Edge deployment means executing the model directly on local hardware like smartphones or smart cameras.

exercises.prompt: Which scenario absolutely requires edge deployment instead of cloud?

options.text: A medical drone inspecting remote areas without cellular coverage
A photo-sharing web app where users upload files to a website
A large language model doing batch translation of historical books

exercises.explain: Without network coverage, cloud services are unreachable. The drone must run edge AI to work.

exercises.prompt: You are designing the safety system for an autonomous drone. If it detects an obstacle, it must brake within 0.05 seconds. The drone flies in rural forests. Which setup should you choose?

options.text: An edge-deployed model running locally on the drone's hardware.
A large cloud model that processes video frames over a satellite connection.
A hybrid model that sends frames to the cloud and waits for approval.
No model; rely on a human remote operator watching a live stream.

exercises.explain: For safety-critical systems that require instant reaction and operate in areas without stable internet, edge deployment is mandatory.
